concept.headline: Classes package data and behavior into reusable object types.
concept.explanation: Crystal classes use initialize to construct instances. Instance variables store object-specific state, and methods expose behavior.
concept.syntax:
class User
  def initialize(@name : String, @score : Int32)
  end

  def label
    "#{@name}: #{@score}"
  end
end

u = User.new("Ari", 10)
puts u.label

demo.intro: Build a User object and call its method.
demo.code:
class User
  def initialize(@name : String, @score : Int32)
  end

  def label
    "#{@name}: #{@score}"
  end
end

u = User.new("Ari", 10)
puts u.label

explanation.misconception: Class definition does not create instances
initialize is invoked automatically by new
Method call uses instance state via @vars

prediction.question:
What prints?

class Box
  def initialize(@n : Int32)
  end
  def double
    @n * 2
  end
end
puts Box.new(4).double

explanation.afterDemo: Classes help organize growing codebases by grouping data with related methods.
keyTakeaways: Objects are concrete runtime values of class types.

practice.prompt: Create class Counter with initialize(@value : Int32) and method inc returning @value + 1. Print Counter.new(9).inc.
practice.solutionCode:
class Counter
  def initialize(@value : Int32)
  end

  def inc
    @value + 1
  end
end

puts Counter.new(9).inc

feedback.successMessage: Great class design and method usage.

summary.title: Classes — Complete
takeaways: Classes model state and behavior together.
